title: Peacock’s Castle and the Neighborhood Photo Project
summary: Mickey, Minnie, and Mole join Izzy the photographer on a journey to the hilltop castle of Peacock the Wizard. Welcomed by the elephant gatekeeper Phillyfoo and guided by Zinna the giraffe, the group explains their mission: a neighborhood photo project for Silver’s third birthday. After photographing the wizard in his cozy fireplace armchair, the team ventures into the forest to capture portraits of their neighbors. They meet Victor the mouse getting dressed after a nap, Hoo-Hoo the owl drying off from a shower, a messy-maned zebra, a camera-shy monkey in a tree, and a dancing elephant named Don-Don. Even the lightning-fast bird Vroom-Vroom can't escape Izzy's lens, ensuring the birthday wall project is a success.

highlightQuote.text: Well, we're going to do a special project with pictures for everyone around this neighborhood, so we're going to print them into a piece of paper and then put them on the wall.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, now it's time for stars and stories. Oh, we didn't turn on the stars. Should we do stars or no stars?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. So, the story is...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The story is... straight story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So, how does our story begin?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. They're going on a journey.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mickey and Minnie and Mole are going on a journey. And where do they start? Whose house do they start at?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Peac- the wizard's house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the castle on top of the hill. So we start there. Mickey and Minnie and Mole arrived at the castle and they knocked on the door. Boom, boom, boom. And a very big creature answered the door this time. Who was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was the gate- it was the gate- it was the gate opener.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The gate opener.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The elephant. The gate opener elephant.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what was the gate opener elephant's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was Phillyfoo. Phillyfoo. And so the gate slowly swung open... creak! And out peeked a very big face with a big trunk and big ears and big, kind, gentle eyes. And Phillyfoo said, 'Hello travelers, welcome to Peacock the Wizard's castle. What is your business here today?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I have to take Peacock the Wizard's picture.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Do you now? Well then, you may come in.' And so Phillyfoo opened the gate the rest of the way and Mickey and Minnie and Mole went inside. And who was carrying the camera?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Izzy the kiddo was with them too and she had the camera. And Mole said, 'Wow, this is a big castle. I- I- I've never been in a castle before. Can you show me around, please?' And who showed Mole around? Was it Izzy the kiddo?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. The show-you-around person was Zinna the Giraffe.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Zinna the giraffe? And she said, 'Welcome little Mole, let me show you some of the fun things we have here. Now, over here is the fireplace where we can get warm when it's cold and there's a little table with lots of games and toys and a nice rug to sit on. And over there is the kitchen where we prepare our great feasts for when Peacock the Wizard has a celebration. And just behind you over there is the feast hall with a big table and lots of chairs where we all sit around eating our delicious feasts. Do you think you'd like to come to a feast here, Mole?' And what did Mole say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know, I just want to go straight to taking the picture for Peacock the Wizard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Zinna the giraffe said, 'Oh yes! Well, I will ring Peacock's bell to see if he is ready to come down.' And so she went over to the wall and pulled a string and high up in the tower, they could hear a little tinkling sound when she pulled the string because at the other end of the string was a little bell. Ding-dong, ding-dong. And then they heard a little... 'Oh! Whoops! My goodness! Oh, someone's waking me up with a bell. What's going on?' And then they heard a clunk-clunk-clunk-clunk-clunk-clunk-clunk as Peacock the Wizard ran down the spiral staircase from the top of her tower all the way down to the ground floor of the castle. And Peacock the Wizard said, 'Oh, I see we have some visitors.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hey, hey, I'm Izzy and this is Baby Austin, this is Jane and Jane has her love you sticker and Austin has a sticker too and I have to take your picture.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'You do?' says Peacock the Wizard. 'Now why is it that you need to take my picture?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because it's Silver's birthday. It's Silver's third birthday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, happy birthday Silver! Wait, Silver's not here. Are you taking the picture to print out and make a beautiful framed portrait of me just for Silver for her birthday?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, we're going to do a special project with pictures for everyone around this neighborhood, so we're going to print them into a piece of paper and then put them on the wall.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Wow, that sounds like an amazing project,' says Peacock the Wizard.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So the first one is you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh! Now where should I pose? Would you like to take my picture here inside or up in my office at the top of the tower, or maybe somewhere outside?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, down at the bottom of this floor.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, okay. Perhaps I'll sit in my favorite armchair right by the fireplace and I'll just put on a nice smile and I'll just fix my feathers a little bit and put on my wizard hat. Okay, now I'm ready. You can take the picture anytime.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I took it right now, it came out great!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, you took the picture already, wonderful! You must be a very good photographer.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then let's visit Victor's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, who's Victor?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor's a mouse. Now let's go up to him. Zoom, zoom, zoom, zoom! Mickey and Minnie and Mole go as fast as you can. You can go walking or running to Victor's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they all ran, ran, ran down the hill out of Peacock the Wizard's castle and down the hill along the road and into the forest and then they went to Victor's house. Now where does Victor live?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He lives next to Peacock the Wizard's house. And then he was there and said, 'Hello everyone!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'Well, what are you here for today?' 'I have to take your picture Victor, Victor.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did Victor say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'You take- you make Victor talk.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I make Victor talk. And Victor said, 'Well, I am not ready to take a picture yet. I need to do something very important first.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'What?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Um, I need to put on some clothes because I also just woke up from a nap. So let's see, I... hmm, what should I wear for my picture? Maybe I'll wear these nice blue shorts and a purple shirt and hmm, oh is my hair messy? I'll comb my hair a little bit, comb-comb-comb-comb. Oh, I think I have some crumbs in my whiskers... wipe-wipe-wipe-wipe-wipe because I had a little muffin before I went to sleep. But now I am ready for my picture.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, I heard a click. Did you take the picture? Yes! Did it come out great? Mm-hm. Oh, very good.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now we have another person, it's Hoo-Hoo the Owl. He lives right next to you, Victor.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh yes, my neighbor Hoo-Hoo. I believe he's home right now.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, he's just right outside in his backyard taking a shower. 'Hello, Hoo-Hoo the Owl!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Who, who is it? Visiting me?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'It's me Izzy, and I have to take your picture.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'But I'm taking a shower just at the moment! Let me get dried off.' And Hoo-Hoo the Owl turned off the shower and then found a towel and then pat-pat-pat-pat-pat got all nice and dry and he said, 'Well, now I'm very clean and ready for my picture.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That was the picture!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh! Do I look good in the picture? Very good.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now let's go off to June-June the Zebra's house. Zoom-zoom-zoom-zoom-zoom!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What kind of a house does June-June the Zebra live in?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. Now June-June the Zebra, we're here!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Neigh! Hello!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'Hello. Well, I'm here to take your picture.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, oh dear. I could use a bit of a hairbrushing if anyone has a brush available.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'I don't think so.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'But my mane is so messy. Oh dear, oh dear. Whatever shall we do about that?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'Well, one of the dolls' combs are available. Here you go, Peacock the Wizard.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Peacock the Wizard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, no. No. Zilla the Zebra. Here you go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Thank you! Comb-comb-comb-comb-comb-comb-comb-comb. Now I am ready for my photoshoot.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's the picture. Lovely.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Lovely.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I put them on the paper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you printed them all out?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, now it's time to go to June-June the Monkey's house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, June-June the Monkey.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Zoom-zoom-zoom-zoom-zoom! We're at June-June the Monkey's house. June-June the Monkey, we're here to take your picture!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Eek-eek-eek!' And June-June ran away. He said, 'I don't want to be in a picture! I'm running up this tree!' And he climbed straight up to the top of the tree and hung out in a branch and said, 'Hee-hee-hee, you can't catch me!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Wait a minute, you took my picture anyway! Hee-hee!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Now let's go to Don-Don the giraffe's house. Don-Don the giraffe must be right across the street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, that's true.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zoom! June-June the giraffe, are you awake?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Zzzzzzzzz...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Are you?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'I'm sleeping. Zzzzzzz...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then we might have to go straight away to Don-Don the elephant's house. Don-Don the elephant, we're home, we're here!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Pffft!' said Don-Don the elephant. 'I'm excited to have my picture taken!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, will you take another one? And then one with my trunk up like this? And then one with my trunk a little bit to the side like that because I think that's a good angle. And then I'll just pose with my- my- my feet up in the air a little bit.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'And then oh, maybe an action shot of me dancing! Stomp, stomp, dance, dance, elephant dance! Oh yes! Now you've got some very good pictures of me.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now let's go to Vroom-Vroom's the bird's house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Vroom-Vroom the bird. 'Tweet-tweet, tweet-tweet, tweet-tweet! Flap-flap-flap-flap-flap-flap. I can fly so fast you'll never catch me on camera. I'm going to zoom right by you. Zoom-flap-flap-flap.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh! You got me! How did you do that?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'I just take your picture.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'You took my picture!' All right, I think we're probably running out of time for Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. But we- are we going to bring them to Silver Cat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, is that all of Stars and Stories? Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now let's do play.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Mole
characters.description: A resourceful, perpetually humming mole with an impossibly long name, this polite and intrepid mariner serves as a bridge between worlds, translating Frasgle’s unique tongue and guiding the way to the hidden Underground Library. Whether managing rocket controls with wide-eyed curiosity, unraveling treehouse mysteries, or sharing the magic of special Legos, he encourages his companions to embrace the unknown between tending his dandelion yard and baking fragrant banana bread. A lover of both wriggly worms and sweet blueberries, he sings whimsical songs of carnabee sharks and drives a noisy dark brown car, remaining firmly hesitant about boarding giant robots even as he coaxes his friends toward bold new adventures.

characters.name: Phillyfoo
characters.description: A large, kind elephant who acts as the gate opener for Peacock's castle.

characters.name: Peacock
characters.description: Residing within the loftiest tower of a hilltop castle, this majestic bird wizard is draped in a vibrant mantle of pink and yellow feathers that shimmer with arcane grace.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Baby Austin
characters.description: A small child with a sticker accompanying the group.

characters.name: Jane
characters.description: Dimetar’s matronly childhood companion is a kindhearted cat and daring ship’s pilot, a friend to Finn, Isabella, and Scratch Cat who navigates the expedition toward Mouse Castle and the road to Cadasori. Her cockpit is a warm sanctuary distinguished by a "love you" sticker and a cabin teeming with whiskered passengers, reflecting the same tenderness she first offered Silver. Though she maintains a bold pace, she is easily swayed by snacks at Seeker’s house or waylaid within the tangled depths of the dark forest. An adventurous spirit with whimsical desires, she currently dreams of a special little hat adorned with rainbow feathers, glitter, and a miniature fishy pond to crown her nautical ensemble.

characters.name: Zinna
characters.description: A giraffe who gives tours of the castle's feast hall, kitchen, and fireplace.

characters.name: Victor
characters.description: Clad in blue shorts and a purple shirt, Lily’s brother is a tiny baby mouse and spirited cosmic stowaway who once served as a royal gardener within the Magic Forest’s glitter-shimmering houses. Now a rugged driver of a tow truck in the big car race, he balances his mechanical duties with a quest for a celestial watering can and the composition of percussive ‘bamp-bam-bum’ beats for space-faring worms. Though he treasures the sparkly art of Uncle Grisco and Haddy, his high-speed adventures often result in muddy messes that necessitate restorative baths, leaving him in singular pursuit of the perfect hat to complete his interstellar ensemble.

characters.name: Hoo-Hoo
characters.description: An owl neighbor of Victor who gets photographed after taking a shower.

characters.name: June-June the Zebra
characters.description: A zebra who needs her mane brushed before having her picture taken.

characters.name: Zilla
characters.description: A zebra who provides a doll's comb to help brush a messy mane.

characters.name: June-June the Monkey
characters.description: A playful monkey who tries to hide in a tree to avoid the camera.

characters.name: Don-Don the Elephant
characters.description: An enthusiastic elephant who loves posing for action shots and dancing.

characters.name: Vroom-Vroom
characters.description: A lightning-fast bird who tries to fly past the camera but gets caught anyway.

places.name: Peacock the Wizard's Castle
places.description: Perched atop a verdant hill, this grand castle serves as a magical sanctuary and the storied venue for the realm’s most spectacular shows. Beyond its formidable gate, the warmth of the kitchen and the echoing splendor of the feast hall provide a staging ground for imaginative quests, while a winding spiral staircase beckons the brave upward toward the spotlight of the wonders that await.

places.name: The Kitchen
places.description: The castle’s grand culinary hall, where Minnie and Mickey orchestrate legendary feasts, is a whimsical blend of stone-walled majesty and cozy clutter. Gleaming utensils and vibrant vegetables spill across the counters, all centered around a peculiar, animated refrigerator that hums with a personality as lively as the banquets prepared within.

places.name: The Feast Hall
places.description: A large room with a big table and chairs for community celebrations.

places.name: The Fireplace
places.description: A cozy spot in the castle with a rug, games, and toys.

places.name: The Forest
places.description: Cloaking the surface of the green planet in a vast mantle of dense greenery, this whispering woodland serves as the lush setting of the 'Forest Friends' chronicle. It is a realm where creatures play eternal hide-and-seek between Hattie’s cozy clinic and the ancestral cradle of Finn’s companions. Along magical trails where Hattie and the Pakruder Worm harvest hat-making ingredients, winding paths veil secret gopher tunnels and hidden mole cottages, forming the essential route toward the wizard's castle. While its sun-drenched threshold remains a sanctuary for the Rainbow Giraffe and Mama Bird, the deeper, darker thickets harbor a somber mystery; it was within these sprawling, uncharted depths—the same eerie expanse where the sick creature was first spotted and Mossy was discovered—that Mole recently vanished into the shadows.

places.name: Victor's House
places.description: Perched atop a mountain peak dusted in shimmering purple glitter, the mouse’s rosy pink house sits nestled within the long shadow of the neighboring Wizard’s castle.

places.name: Hoo-Hoo's Backyard
places.description: The place where the owl takes his outdoor shower.

places.name: June-June's Tree
places.description: A tall tree where the monkey hides during the photoshoot.

places.name: Vroom-Vroom's House
places.description: The home of the fast-flying bird.
